messages.content:
你了解这篇论文吗   Hamming-shifting graph of genomic short
reads: Efficient construction and its
application for compression
作为AI语言模型，我可以读取并了解论文的大意。这篇论文介绍了一种名为“Hamming-shifting graph”的数据结构，用于高效地构建基因组短序列的图形表示，并进行压缩以减少存储空间。作者们还通过实验证明了该方法的有效性和可扩展性，表明其在基因组学和其他领域中具有潜力。
能否详细介绍下这篇论文的详细做法

当处理基因组短序列时，传统的字符串匹配方法会面临存储和计算上的困难，因为基因组短序列具有高度重叠和相似性。因此，Hamming-shifting graph能够在基因组短序列之间构建一种图形表示。

Hamming-shifting graph的构建流程如下：

1.创建一个起点节点，该节点代表长度为k的短序列中的第一个k-mer。

2.每次添加一个长度为k的短序列时，从第一个k-mer开始，对每个相邻k-mer计算其Hamming距离（即两个k-mer之间不同字符的数量）。如果两个k-mer之间的Hamming距离为1，则在它们之间添加一条边。如果存在多个可选k-mer，则选择具有最少边的k-mer。

3.继续添加短序列，直到构建出整个Hamming-shifting graph。

这种方法的关键点是将每个短序列表示为一个图的路径，而不是一个字符串。这将允许我们有效地存储和处理基因组短序列，并在必要时轻松进行压缩。

在实验中，作者们证明了Hamming-shifting graph的有效性和可扩展性，并将其用于数据压缩。结果表明，该方法可以比传统的压缩方法实现更好的压缩效果，并且对于大型基因组短序列的处理，其性能优于其他现有方法。
这篇论文，我发一些片段，你帮我翻译成中文
当然，我可以帮您翻译论文的片段，您可以告诉我您需要翻译的内容，并提供相关的上下文，我会尽力帮您了解其含义。

If two reads share the same k-minimizer, they will have a common substring of a length at
least >= k. We use these minimizer substrings to index the reads into blocks. Then only those
reads in the same block are compared to get their mismatching distance (i.e., their weights) for
the construction of the HaS-graph. Here, minimizer is used as the anchor to achieve a fast
alignment and comparison. The details of the procedure are as follows:
如果两条读取共享相同的k-minimizer，则它们将具有长度至少大于等于k的公共子串。我们使用这些minimizer子串将读取索引为块。然后，只有在同一块中的读取才进行比较，以获取它们的不匹配距离（即它们的权重），用于构建HaS-graph。在这里，minimizer被用作锚点来实现快速对齐和比较。具体过程如下：

步骤1：识别所有读取的k-minimizer，并将具有相同minimizer的读取聚类到同一块中；
• 步骤2：在每个块中，根据minimizer在升序位置上的位置对读取进行排序；

Step 3: For each read, search its neighbouring reads in its block to construct its edges. To
avoid duplicate edges, it is only compared to the reads ahead of it.
The clustering results, including the number of blocks and the sizes of blocks, are sensitive
to the setting of k. Assume that two reads have only one pair of mismatching bases, and suppose they are clustered into two different blocks when k = d. It is the case that they can be clustered into the same block when k = d − 1. Therefore, some good edges may be missed if only
one setting of k is used.
To overcome this sensitivity, we use multiple settings of k 2 {k1, k2, . . ., kn} and the above
search is carried out across n rounds independently. Moreover, k-maximizer can be used independently to search the edges as the above search procedure except that k-maximizer is identified in Step 1. Therefore, we can find the weight-lightest edges through making use of multiple
minimizers and maximizers, with a high probability.
For large blocks, the computational complexity involved in Step 3 can still be high. To handle such cases, we limit the number of reads for the search.
In each round of edge construction (with respect to a specific k setting), the edges are sorted
by their weight and stored. We note that the search space has slight effects on the compression
ratio (as we demonstrate in the S1 File).

步骤3：对于每个读取，在其块中搜索相邻的读取以构建其边缘。为了避免重复的边缘，只与其前面的读取进行比较。
聚类结果，包括块的数量和块的大小，对于k的设置非常敏感。假设两个读取仅有一个不匹配碱基对，并且当k = d时，假设它们被聚类到两个不同的块中。当k = d-1时，它们可以聚类到同一个块中。因此，如果只使用一个k的设置，可能会错过一些好的边缘。
为了克服这种敏感性，我们使用多个k设置{k1、k2、...、kn}，并且以上搜索是独立地在n轮中进行的。此外，k-maximizer可以独立地用于搜索边缘，方法与上述搜索过程相同，只是在步骤1中识别k-maximizer而已。因此，我们可以通过利用多个minimizer和maximizer来找到权重最轻的边缘，从而具有较高的概率。
对于大型块，步骤3涉及的计算复杂度仍然很高。为了处理这种情况，我们限制了搜索的读取数量。
在每个边缘构建的回合中（针对特定的k设置），对边缘按权重进行排序并进行存储。我们注意到搜索空间对压缩比有轻微的影响（如我们在S1文件中演示的那样）。

We employ parallel computing for the edge construction steps. Specifically in Step 1, the
identification of each minimizer and/or maximizer is conducted in parallel for each read.
Next, the sorting of reads is conducted in parallel for each block. Finally, in Step 3, we exploit
one last piece of parallelisation. For each read, in parallel, we independently search its neighbouring reads in its block. Then those edges are stored into different files according to
their weight. Therefore, the edges can be sorted by their weight after the edge construction
step.
我们对边缘构建步骤采用并行计算。具体来说，在步骤1中，对于每个读取，每个minimizer和/或maximizer的识别都是并行进行的。接下来，对于每个块，读取的排序也是并行的。最后，在步骤3中，我们利用最后一块并行处理。对于每个读取，我们并行地独立搜索其在块中的相邻读取。然后，根据其权重，将这些边缘存储到不同的文件中。因此，边缘可以在边缘构建步骤之后按其权重进行排序。

Construction of minimum spanning forests
We employ the well known Kruskal’s algorithm to find the MSF. The steps are:
• Step 1: Set each node of the graph as a separate tree;
• Step 2: Load edges from the disk in the order of the edge weights from the smallest to
largest;
• Step 3: For an edge, if it can connect two different trees, i.e., it does not introduce a cycle,
then add the edge to the forest and combine the two trees to form one tree.
In implementation, the disjoint-set data structure [18] is used to check whether two nodes
are in the same tree or in two different trees.

最小生成森林构建
我们采用广为人知的Kruskal算法来找到MSF。步骤如下：
• 步骤1：将图的每个节点设置为单独的树；
• 步骤2：按从最小到最大的边权重顺序从磁盘加载边缘；
• 步骤3：对于一条边，如果它可以连接两个不同的树，即它不会引入循环，则将该边加入森林并合并两个树以形成一个树。
在实现中，使用不相交集数据结构[18]来检查两个节点是否在同一棵树中或在两棵不同的树中。

Encoding reads on the tree one-by-one through depth-first traversal
Given an MST, any node can be set as its root node. The encoded string of the root node is
itself. For every non-root node, it is encoded by using its parent node as reference. The
encoded string of a non-root read includes two parts: shifting substring and mismatch information. The shifting substring is followed by the mismatch information. For each mismatch
base, the position (delta encoded) and the base are concatenated as a substring. For example,
assume that the current read is “ATGCAT”, its parent read is “GCATCC” and the shift offset is
2, then the encoded string of the current read is “AT2G”. An extra bit is used to indicate the
shifting direction (left or right direction).
Depth-first search (DFS) is taken to travel the MST for encoding the reads one-by-one. The
encoded strings are stored in a text file line-by-line following the DFS node visiting order.
When a leaf node is met, a backtracking step number is stored in a separate line to tell where
the next node to visit is. This information is necessary for recovering the tree structure in
decompression. It is easy to distinguish the backtracking step number and the encoded string,
since at least a letter is contained in the encoded string but only digital numbers are stored in
the line of the backtracking step number.

通过深度优先遍历树来逐个编码读取
对于给定的MST，任何节点都可以被设置为其根节点。根节点的编码字符串是它本身。对于每个非根节点，它通过使用其父节点作为参考进行编码。非根读取的编码字符串包括两个部分：移位子串和不匹配信息。移位子串后跟不匹配信息。对于每个不匹配碱基，位置（经过增量编码）和碱基被串联成子串。例如，假设当前读取为“ATGCAT”，其父读取为“GCATCC”，移位偏移量为2，则当前读取的编码字符串为“AT2G”。使用额外的位来指示移位方向（左或右方向）。
通过深度优先搜索（DFS）遍历MST以逐个编码读取。编码字符串按照DFS节点访问顺序逐行存储在文本文件中。当遇到叶节点时，在单独的一行中存储回溯步骤编号，以告诉下一个要访问的节点在哪里。这些信息对于在解压缩时恢复树结构是必要的。很容易区分回溯步骤编号和编码字符串，因为编码字符串中至少包含一个字母，但仅数字数字存储在回溯步骤编号行中。

Data structures and other details in the encoding
Our method first detects all duplicate reads using 31-minimizer. Those reads sharing the same
minimizer at the same position are clustered into the same block. Duplicate reads are detected
in each block. As a minimizer is identified from two strands (original strand and its reverse
complementary strand), duplicate reads are also distinguished into two types. This duplicate
information is stored in the final compressed file.
We use the following five data streams to encode an MSF:
• Stream 1: a text file to store the encoded strings of all nodes (under the DFS order) and the
backtracking step numbers (in text numbers).
• Stream 2: a bit stream to tell the shifting direction of an encoded string.
• Stream 3: a bit stream to tell whether a read is encoded on its RC (reverse complementary)
strand.

编码中的数据结构和其他细节
我们的方法首先使用31-minimizer检测所有重复读数。共享相同minimizer且位置相同的读数被聚类到同一块中。在每个块中检测重复读取。由于minimizer从两个链上识别（原始链和它的反向互补链），因此还将重复读取分为两种类型。此重复信息存储在最终压缩文件中。
我们使用以下五个数据流来对MSF进行编码：
• 第1个数据流：文本文件，用于存储所有节点（根据DFS顺序）和回溯步骤编号（以文本数字形式）的编码字符串。
• 第2个数据流：位流，用于指示编码字符串的移位方向。
• 第3个数据流：位流，用于指示读取是否在其RC（反向互补）链上编码。

Stream 4: a bit stream to tell whether a node has duplicate reads;
• Stream 5: if a node has duplicate, two integers are stored. One is for the number of normal
duplicate reads and the other is for the number of RC duplicate reads.
All of these data streams are compressed by BSC (http://libbsc.com).
For the compression of paired-end reads, we need to store the pairing information. After
vertically concatenating the two FASTQ files, the minimum spanning forest construction follows the same steps as for constructing MSFs from single-end reads. For each read, including
duplicate reads, its visiting order is recorded in the DFS traversal. For each pair of reads, the
distance in the visiting order after removing the processed reads is stored. In our implementation, the binary indexed tree [19] is used to compute the distance of a pair of reads (see details
in the S1 File). An extra bit is used to record the reads order in the pair, i.e., whether the first
read of the pair is from the first (_1) file or from the second (_2) file. The distance data stream
is compressed by LZMA (http://www.7zip.org).

第4个数据流：位流，用于指示节点是否有重复读取;
• 第5个数据流：如果节点有重复，则存储两个整数。一个是用于普通重复读取的数量，另一个是用于RC重复读取的数量。
所有这些数据流都通过BSC（http://libbsc.com）进行压缩。
对于成对末端读取的压缩，我们需要存储配对信息。在垂直连接两个FASTQ文件后，通过最小生成森林的构建与从单端读取中构建MSF的步骤相同。对于每个读取，包括重复读取，在DFS遍历中记录
